C[SunPin: I feel stronger when I take a few minutes to play out several different possibilities. Haven't been able to do that in my five years of playing until I came here. Maybe it was available elsewhere but nothing happens the day before it is supposed to happen.
afar: One big thing is, I find my judgement of the situation can change a lot with a few minutes to contemplate it
afar: I'm not yet sure if I'm convincing myself bad situations are okay, or if my initial judgement was wrong

SunPin: In the opening of a handicap game, my local teacher tells me to ""go opposite."" He is an old Korean and our only common language is baduk. I defeated a three Kyu with this advice at the KGS iron man. KGS players say to pincer but I Get torn apart when that happens.
afar: We'll meet again, I'm sure
afar: I remember I never wanted to pincer, for the same reasons, when I was around your level
afar: But then I tried it in one game...and it worked!
afar: And I tried it some more
afar: And I realised everything I had been missing out on
afar: So...just my own anecdote on pincering :)
C[SunPin: Sorry about yesterday, my niece and nephew arrived. I thought I'd have an hour and I only had a few minutes. I gave them their first set. My 9 x 9 board and about 50 stones of each color.
C[SunPin: They are ages five and eight, respectively.
afar: Hey, no worries, it's a correspondence game :)
C[SunPin: I made a 50 K mistake.
C[SunPin: A free one as well. I am trying my best though. Need to keep this redaction to a minimum.
C[afar: That's the spirit. Everyone makes mistakes, the ones who keep their heads are definitely stronger than the ones that give up though
C[SunPin: Well.  Talk about a cycle of violence.  I'm going to put a hurting on some people after this one.  :-)

C[SunPin: Sure enough, it seems that my slow play on nova is helping me on KGS. I am seeing more moves ahead. I'm seeing a different board than I was a few weeks ago. Getting killed here but that's a different story. :-)
C[afar: Are you sure you meant to play this one? :-)
SunPin: ouch. I see that. I was on my way out the door when I set up the moves. I'm going to request an undo. If you do not grant it, I'm okay with th
afar: I take the view it's the best way to learn
C[SunPin: Absolutely. Why on earth would you apologize?
afar: Would you like to review sometime?
SunPin: Yes. Let me know when you want to do this. What's your one sentence impression of my gameplay?
afar: I think...one of two things. Either 'misjudged size', or 'hurting yourself'
afar: It's interesting to note that in every corner of the board, w ended up with more than b
SunPin: Okay. I look forward to the details. Just send me a message when you want to start.
afar: That means your stones didn't work together enough :)
afar: Anyway, I'm not free today or probably tomorrow, but maybe at the weekend?
afar: Or probably any other time I'm online other than the next couple of days
afar: Well, I have to go for now, but I expect I'll be around. I'll message you sometime in a couple of days about it

afar: I think I got rather lucky on the right
gloooomph: not sure what made me think p17 was the right move
gloooomph: well according to ogs ai I was behind so being brave was in a way the right idea
gloooomph: that may not have been the right way though
afar: I actually thought you were fine if you just defended
afar: I hadn't actually counted, I was just following my zen instincts of never invading moyos
gloooomph: my count actually said I might be ok but you never know with moyos
afar: I actually thought it was the kind of position where a bot would be much better at invading and probably fine, but I wouldn't be
gloooomph: I just thought p17 was much less risky than it was
gloooomph: I felt bad you got o18 because I felt entitled to that so I lashed out
gloooomph: the ai seems to think o18 might actually have been a mistake though
afar: I'm seeing what katago thinks about the actual score
afar: Looks like katago thinks white is 10 points ahead at the point O18 is played, but it wants to fight by playing P8 right away
gloooomph: it looks like it wants to do that pretty much the whole game
gloooomph: or at least for the short time it was there
gloooomph: I guess it does make a lot of sense looking at it now
afar: I thought you could fight and be fine, but I assume that turns out not to be true
gloooomph: it looks like it's not a disaster for me if it happens
gloooomph: seems like you can get some points but it thinks doing something on the bottom is bigger most of the time
gloooomph: I wondered if it might say after the game we should always be playing on the bottom
afar: But I thought it would probably either say we should always do it, or totally never do it ever
gloooomph: yeah I don't get it
gloooomph: sometimes it doesn't seem to care about territory and stones living at all
gloooomph: then it goes and calls you an idiot for not giving up sente to try and rescue some worthless stones
gloooomph: i guess here your position had some weaknesses in which makes it a good idea to try and exploit
afar: It does look like the reasons it thought white was ahead were not remotely reasons I was cognisant of
afar: In the early game in particular, it always wants to do clever invasions using things like the peep at P16
gloooomph: I guess that's a tricky move
gloooomph: it seems to think we should both have spent a lot more energy on the moyo on the left
gloooomph: I assume you're playing Zherui tomorrow
gloooomph: just get 7 points more than last time
afar: It's Japanese rules so I hope he's white to start with a disdvantage
gloooomph: you think white has a disadvantage?
gloooomph: also, is this meant to be japanese rules? I started this challenge with aga rules
gloooomph: maybe my shape would have been better under japanese rules
afar: Yes, it's supposed to be Japanese
afar: Did you see Katago found a mistake in the optimal play for 7x7?
gloooomph: I didn't know there was an optimal play for 7x7
afar: There is a ""strongly believed to be optimal"" set of lines, leading to white losing by 9 points
gloooomph: actually yeah I do remember seeing that thing on senseis library where they said they had it
afar: It turns out white needs 9 points komi only in area counting, under Japanese rules white only needs 8 komi
afar: I don't understand why but it's still interesting :p
gloooomph: well it's normal for the score to differ by one point isn't it?
afar: Right, but that didn't happen in any of the previously-analysed lines
afar: Katago finds a ridiculous way to get aji from nowhere
gloooomph: I never believed they had really found the optimal play anyway
afar: Well, I'm going to have lunch. Good luck with your own game tomorrow and have a nice weekend
gloooomph: ok, have a nice lunch

C[Uberdude: I often play 3-3 as white in diagonal openings, the idea being hard for you to make a moyo and the territories will be small so komi is relatively bigger so this gives me nice points.
C[Uberdude: This is a little slack, as c13 is then rather nice to split the board up and develop on both sides. c8 pincer more active.
C[Uberdude: I think d12 is more normal here, then you get the corner which is always nice for points and base.
C[Uberdude: I also considered d11, but decided against it as you can split at d9 and d15 makes your group strong so not so much point taking a liberty.
C[Uberdude: Often black puts in the e16 e17 c16 d17 exchange first to slow white down a bit and make eye at c15.
C[Uberdude: Now I can answer e16 at e15.
C[Uberdude: My original plan with the j16 press was to make you low and overconcentrated with o17 and take sente for o4, but then decided I needed another move...
C[Uberdude: ... so was happy you played gote here, maybe you can approach r3 now, I can't kill you, unclear how much I can profit from enclosing.
C[Uberdude: I wasn't sure which side to push, but went for this one to get more side points, whereas r10 q9 q11 o10 p11 n8 makes more strength at p11 area which seemed wasteful with m16.
C[Uberdude: Interesting, I was hoping to play b5 myself soonish and was only expecting b6 for you which keeps sente but gets less.
C[Uberdude: I think this is the first mayor mistake as it isn't sente, you need to split at j11.
C[Uberdude: I decided to be brave/foolish and decided this wasn't sente (at least a ko) and h11 means n10 is attack plus building middle.
C[Uberdude: Annoying sabaki technique, I wondered if I should have just extended but then decided hane was still probably best.
C[Uberdude: Would be exciting if you played in top left and I tried to kill! Probably rather ambitious for me.
Uberdude: But as I didn't see a clean kill for you at top I decided I could at the least attack and profit in sente and then come back to answer at top, so I think this is good.
C[Uberdude: Nice for black to answer in profity way

C[Uberdude: I think this is slack, I don't think it is 100% needed to defend against L3 but wasn't sure of my reading and thought I was quite far ahead so could afford it. But I was counting middle as mine and it isn't really. Also considered s17, but maybe h9 honte is best.
Uberdude: Or is j9 best there perhaps
C[Uberdude: Only kikashi before defending if really is sente =) g6 directly seems fine, and then yes I was planning to f11 but black can be happy to have broken middle.
